purpose: Documentation continuity + report-generation system: frontmatter schema, repo-index generator (STATE/catalog/BLOCKERS with anti-stale check), curated blocker register, topic map-of-content hubs, and a binding cross-agent rule.
scope: Repo-wide documentation infrastructure. Claude and Codex are teammates on one shared corpus (no per-doc owner). No science artifacts, solver outputs, scheduler state, registry, or external cfd-modeling-tools files modified.
teammate_model: No per-document owner field. Traceability via task ID; the BOARD Owner column is only a live active-task lock, not authorship.
modified_docs: AGENTS.md (Documentation continuity and index protocol section)

verification.generation: python3 tools/docs/build_repo_index.py -> indexed 974 docs; 105 board rows; 10 blockers

open_questions_for_user: Install a git pre-commit or nightly cron hook to auto-run the generator?
Backfill frontmatter on high-traffic legacy docs to raise priority-field coverage, or leave adoption incremental?
